---
title: "phynotype formulas"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{phynotype formulas}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(comment = "#>")
library(phynotype)
```

# Overview

This vignette collects the formulas implemented by `phynotype` so the
documentation matches the code path used by `validate()`, `feature_importance()`,
and the stability helpers.

# Internal validation metrics

## Silhouette width

For observation \eqn{i},

$$
s(i) = \frac{b(i) - a(i)}{\max\{a(i), b(i)\}},
$$

where \eqn{a(i)} is the mean distance from \eqn{i} to its own cluster and
\eqn{b(i)} is the minimum mean distance from \eqn{i} to any other cluster.

The reported metric is the average silhouette width across all observations.

## Calinski-Harabasz index

$$
\mathrm{CH} = \frac{\mathrm{BSS}/(k - 1)}{\mathrm{WSS}/(n - k)}.
$$

Here, \eqn{k} is the number of clusters and \eqn{n} is the number of
observations.

The code computes:

- \eqn{\mathrm{BSS}} as the between-cluster sum of squares,
- \eqn{\mathrm{WSS}} as the within-cluster sum of squares.

## Davies-Bouldin index

$$
\mathrm{DB} = \frac{1}{k} \sum_{j=1}^k \max_{l \ne j}
\frac{s_j + s_l}{d(\mu_j, \mu_l)}.
$$

The implementation uses the mean Euclidean scatter within each cluster and the
Euclidean distance between cluster centroids.

## Total within-cluster sum of squares

$$
\mathrm{WSS} = \sum_{j=1}^k \sum_{i \in C_j} \|x_i - \mu_j\|^2.
$$

This is the raw k-means objective used as a lower-is-better internal criterion.

# External validation metrics

## Adjusted Rand index

For two partitions \eqn{x} and \eqn{y}, the adjusted Rand index compares the
observed agreement in pairwise assignments against its chance expectation.

The implementation follows the standard contingency-table formula.

## Normalized mutual information

$$
\mathrm{NMI}(x, y) = \frac{I(x;y)}{\sqrt{H(x)H(y)}}.
$$

Here, \eqn{I} is mutual information and \eqn{H} is Shannon entropy.

# Stability metrics

## Bootstrap ARI

For bootstrap repetition \eqn{b}, the package:

1. draws a bootstrap sample from the rows of the input data,
2. refits the clustering model,
3. computes the adjusted Rand index on the sampled observations only.

The reported `bootstrap_ari` is the mean across repetitions.

## Pairwise partition agreement

For two candidate partitions \eqn{x} and \eqn{y},

$$
\mathrm{PPA}(x, y) = \frac{1}{\binom{n}{2}}
\sum_{i < j} I\{(x_i = x_j) = (y_i = y_j)\}.
$$

This is the agreement rate over all observation pairs.

# Feature importance

Permutation feature importance in `phynotype` compares the score on the original
data to the score after a feature is permuted.

For instability,

$$
FI_j = \frac{1}{M} \sum_{m=1}^{M}
\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{n} I\{\hat c_i \ne f(X^{(m)}_{\pi(j)})_i\}.
$$

For silhouette loss,

$$
FI_j = \frac{1}{M}\sum_{m=1}^{M}\left[S(f, X) - S(f, X^{(m)}_{\pi(j)})\right].
$$

For total within-cluster sum of squares,

$$
FI_j = \frac{1}{M}\sum_{m=1}^{M}\left[W(f, X^{(m)}_{\pi(j)}) - W(f, X)\right].
$$

The score definitions above match the implementation in `feature_importance()`.
